steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence says New Mexico joined the list of US ______ to have been led by a woman. The blank is a plural noun phrase — it refers to multiple states and territories collectively. No possession is involved, so no apostrophe is needed.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — states and territory's: adds an apostrophe to "territory's," making it possessive. Nothing is owned here. ✗
  • Choice B — state's and territories': marks both nouns as possessive. Still no possession in the sentence. ✗
  • Choice C — states and territories: plain plurals, no apostrophes. Fits the sentence perfectly — New Mexico joined a list of places (states and territories) that have been led by a woman. ✓
  • Choice D — state's and territories: makes "state's" possessive but leaves "territories" as a plain plural. Inconsistent and still wrong. ✗
That is the correct answer.
